jokes.text: Will talking sweetly to Lisa on phone while being attacked by dog Phoebe, with Carlton screaming in background
jokes.setup: Will trying to have romantic phone conversation
jokes.punchline: Chaos with dog attack and Carlton screaming disrupts the mood
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with nice contrast between Will's smooth talk and chaotic reality

jokes.text: Let me see you caress the syrup with your tongue
jokes.setup: Artist directing Will during portrait session
jokes.punchline: Overly sensual direction for eating breakfast
jokes.explanation: Creepy artist trope executed well with memorable phrasing

jokes.text: Why don't you let me see you caress a Tic Tac with your tongue?
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic counter-suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will comeback - specific, visual, and character-appropriate sass

jokes.text: Well, look, why don't you get up out of my character while I'm eating
jokes.setup: Artist talking about Will's face having 'character'
jokes.punchline: Will plays on 'character' meaning both personality and space
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that's quintessentially Will - clever, quick, and street-smart

jokes.text: Hilary explains she's staying because her house is dark with no lights, then reveals the phone doesn't work when told to call electric company
jokes.punchline: Logical solution fails because phone also doesn't work without power
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - she's genuinely clueless about basic life functions

jokes.text: Well, I don't know, I suppose when it's finished, you'll dust it.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey asks if he'll be involved in the family portrait
jokes.punchline: Philip reduces him to just the help who dusts
jokes.explanation: Classic class dynamic humor between Philip and Geoffrey - predictable but well-executed

jokes.text: Yes, and I'm going to a Quayle in '96 Tupperware Party
jokes.punchline: Carlton's bizarre combination of Dan Quayle politics and Tupperware
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Carlton line - combines his political conservatism with domestic activities

jokes.text: You out of the mix, man. You're married
jokes.setup: Will asking why he doesn't know about party
jokes.punchline: Being in relationship = social death
jokes.explanation: Relatable relationship humor, though fairly standard

jokes.text: Extended bee metaphor sequence about relationships and parties
jokes.setup: Jazz warns about girls swarming like bees
jokes.punchline: Escalating bee puns until Will gets confused
jokes.explanation: Clever extended metaphor that gets a bit convoluted but has good payoff

jokes.text: Look at how cute... You are a lucky man
jokes.setup: Jazz starts to comment on Lisa
jokes.punchline: Catches himself and changes tone when she appears
jokes.explanation: Standard 'speaking of the devil' moment

jokes.text: How come we don't never do that in our nighties?
jokes.punchline: Will's selfish focus on the sexy part
jokes.explanation: Typical male perspective humor, well-delivered by Will

jokes.text: Yeah, they playing against the... uh... The, uh... Uh, the Twins.
jokes.setup: Will awkwardly lying about basketball game details
jokes.punchline: Names baseball team instead of basketball team
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Will's lie falling apart under pressure with sports mix-up

jokes.text: Hey, Ricky, Lucy let you out?
jokes.punchline: I Love Lucy reference about controlling spouse
jokes.explanation: Standard relationship joke with dated reference

jokes.text: Last I heard, Jewel was patting you on your head and tossing you a treat
jokes.punchline: Will compares the other guy to a pet dog
jokes.explanation: Good comeback with vivid imagery

jokes.text: It's all about letting her know who wears the protective cup in the family
jokes.punchline: Sports equipment instead of 'pants' in the relationship
jokes.explanation: Clever twist on common phrase with sports imagery

jokes.text: Do you have, like, a really fat twin sister?
jokes.punchline: Suggests they look like a heavier version of someone else
jokes.explanation: Mean-spirited joke that relies on body shaming

jokes.text: Ten Tummy Tina?
jokes.punchline: Cruel nickname reveals their history
jokes.explanation: Memorable but cruel nickname that establishes backstory

jokes.text: Well, but then, two months ago, you were still up
jokes.setup: Will saying he would have been interested two months ago
jokes.punchline: Tina points out he was heavier then too
jokes.explanation: Excellent comeback that turns his weight comment against him with wordplay

jokes.text: Um... See, what had happened at first was...
jokes.setup: Will caught at party by Lisa
jokes.punchline: Classic stalling phrase when caught in a lie
jokes.explanation: Recognizable panic response, relatable but not original

jokes.text: ...but then a sniper came. And... The coach had got shot
jokes.setup: Will trying to explain why he's not at basketball game
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous action movie scenario
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation shows Will's desperation

jokes.text: by now I'd have been up in this party in my drawers doing the Tootsie Roll
jokes.punchline: Specific image of his past party behavior
jokes.explanation: Vivid, character-specific imagery of Will's wild past

jokes.text: I'm at Hoochie Phi Hoochie and I ain't having no fun
jokes.punchline: Made-up fraternity name with obvious implications
jokes.explanation: Brilliant fake fraternity name that's highly quotable

jokes.text: Miss Nightie-Wearing, Popcorn-Popping, Dancing-To-Janet... Well-At-Least-That's-What-I'm-Going Tell-my-Boyfriend, Little-Party-Freak?
jokes.punchline: Will's elaborate callback to her earlier description with added implications
jokes.explanation: Good callback with escalating accusations, though verbose

jokes.text: Hush, puppy
jokes.punchline: Will's condescending dismissal
jokes.explanation: Quick dismissal with slight cleverness in the phrasing

jokes.text: Um, we was talking about this movie we had seen. Um, the movie we just came from... it was about a linebacker and a fraternity. You know, and Larry Fishburne played the dean
jokes.setup: Being caught in compromising position with Ashley
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous and specific movie plot
jokes.explanation: Standard 'bad lie when caught' scenario, too elaborate to be believable

jokes.text: He was a linebacker, Uncle Phil
jokes.setup: Philip calling the boy a fullback
jokes.punchline: Ashley correcting football position while missing bigger issue
jokes.explanation: Perfect teenage mindset - correcting trivial details while in serious trouble

jokes.text: If all your friends jumped off... the damn MTV building, would you do it?
jokes.setup: Ashley saying all her friends were at party
jokes.punchline: Classic parental comeback updated with 90s MTV reference
jokes.explanation: Clever update of classic parental saying with contemporary reference

jokes.text: At a fraternity house playing touch football in the bedroom
jokes.punchline: Obvious sexual euphemism delivered sarcastically
jokes.explanation: Clear innuendo that works for both adult and young audiences

jokes.text: So long ago, how could you remember?
jokes.setup: Vivian saying she's trying to remember being 15
jokes.punchline: Philip joking about her age
jokes.explanation: Standard husband teasing wife about age

jokes.text: Miss Ashley and I are about the same height... it would be a simple matter of painting my head on her body
jokes.setup: Geoffrey suggesting Ashley be removed from portrait
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and creepy substitution plan
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully weird Geoffrey moment with disturbing implications

jokes.text: When we find Ashley, she's reimbursing me for gas
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's concern about expense reimbursement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey - practical concerns during family drama

jokes.text: I'd probably check into the Four Seasons and get a massage
jokes.setup: Where would Ashley go if she ran away
jokes.punchline: Hilary's luxury approach to rebellion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary - can't conceive of roughing it even while running away

jokes.text: That big vein in his neck looked like an anaconda
jokes.punchline: Specific visual comparison
jokes.explanation: Vivid, memorable metaphor for Philip's anger

jokes.text: If you're referring to the size of my endowment, it's almost as large as Hilary's
jokes.punchline: Carlton innocently mentions inheritance while creating sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - innocent comment creates hilarious misunderstanding

jokes.text: God, I hope you talking about money
jokes.punchline: Will's horrified realization of the innuendo
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction that completes the double entendre joke

jokes.text: They're like piranha, Ashley. And you're 110 pounds of teenage chum
jokes.punchline: Vivid predator/prey metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant, memorable metaphor that's both funny and effective

jokes.text: A hundred and eight and I'm not as innocent as I look. All right, I am
jokes.punchline: Ashley corrects weight then contradicts herself about innocence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ashley's teenage bravado vs reality

jokes.text: The Lord is my shepherd...
jokes.punchline: Starts reciting death psalm
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic exaggeration of facing Philip's anger

jokes.text: call your sister and tell her she's about to lose her only child
jokes.punchline: Threatens to essentially disown her
jokes.explanation: Typical parental hyperbole that feels authentic to the character

jokes.text: they're actually gonna let Ashley eat tomorrow
jokes.punchline: Implies she was starved as punishment
jokes.explanation: Mild exaggeration of strict punishment

jokes.text: And I should be wearing some kind of hat
jokes.punchline: Random hat requirement
jokes.explanation: Random absurdist touch typical of Will's humor

jokes.text: Like, take this jacket you're wearing. What about this jacket? I love it
jokes.setup: Will saying truth hurts, using jacket as example
jokes.punchline: Lisa expects insult but gets compliment
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection that subverts expected insult with sweetness
